data.narrative:
Frankenburg Technologies is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised €40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of four same-sector, same-stage private companies: Scale AI ($29.0B), Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). This peer set spans a wide range, from $150M to $29.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in the defense tech market at early stages.

The peer-median method is applicable and yields an implied valuation of $400M. This represents the midpoint across the four comparable companies. Given the limited availability of triangulation methods in the current analysis, the median valuation of $400M serves as the primary anchor point.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with $400M as the median. Key uncertainties include the substantial dispersion within the peer set—particularly the $29.0B Scale AI outlier, which may reflect sector-specific factors or differing risk profiles not fully captured by stage and sector alone. The narrow methodological base (one method applied) also limits confidence; additional approaches such as revenue multiples or DCF analysis would strengthen conviction in any specific valuation point within the derived range.
